channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-9633] Add kernel-doc style comments to All lustre Code describing function & parameters.
item.description:
Presently not all of lustre code is covered with structured documentation or kernel-doc style comments describing functions and parameters or types. This EPIC/Story/Bug aims to add these remaining coverage. Once this is done, it can then be used to create a documentation as docbook or more latest sphinx format.

Sample Kernel-doc functional documentation format:
/**
 * foobar() - Brief description of foobar.
 * @arg: Description of argument of foobar.
 *
 * Longer description of foobar.
 *
 * Return: Description of return value of foobar.
 */
int foobar(int arg)
item.summary: Add kernel-doc style comments to All lustre Code describing function & parameters.

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

Subject: LU-9633 osp: Add kernel doc style for osp (5)

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60253/
Subject: LU-9633 osp: Add kernel doc style for osp (4)
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 0b9e6cedea6a229340acffb876a409be725a4728

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/60252/
Subject: LU-9633 osp: Add kernel doc style for osp (3)
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: cea09299d4529fecf6612626a5b5225bf34aad24

Andreas, future going I will use their respective tickets. Separating with ticket does make it easier to track. Unfortunately, as you put, few subsystem are done. Which I will link & close the ticket. Rest will be done under their respective Jira.

arshad512, I see that patch https://review.whamcloud.com/60159 ("LU-9633 osp: Add kernel doc style for osp (1)") landed under this ticket instead of LU-9637 "{{Add kernel-doc style comments for subsystem osp }}". While not critical, it would be better to submit the subsystem patches under the right ticket to simplify tracking of when a subsystem has been completed, instead of having a few hundred patches using LU-9633.

In any case, the tickets linked to this Epic should be closed when all of the files related to that subsystem have landed (regardless of which LU ticket was used), so that it is easier to see the current status.

Thanks.

"Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57725
Subject: LU-9633: Add kernel doc style for lustre/llite/*.c (1/2)

Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: ea73a80eb2db7250db434a4f9b7c79b4735464e5
Before we start I pushed the sphinix port to the linux lustre client first. I plan to move the upstream client out of the staging tree so the docs can be tested to see how they turn out.
James, apologies for the delay response. I completely missed your comment until today. Yes, I do have couple of Sphinx related patch's to get started. I should be pushing it very soon.
Arshad are you still interested in this work?
Arshad thanks for taking this up. I have not had the cycles to do this.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: Add kernel-doc style comments to All lustre Code.
